**NH REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER**
**FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 9th**
|
---
**_A Note From Jeanie_**
**Dear Derek,**

**It is with great sadness we learned this week of Senator Sam Cataldo's passing.**
**
**
**I had the opportunity to serve with Sam in the Senate. He was a kind and caring colleague and friend who will be sorely missed.**
****

**Even after Sam left the Senate, he was still actively involved in New Hampshire politics, attending Republican Committee meetings, Republican events, and our Executive Board meetings. One of the last times I spoke with Sam he expressed his desire to run for office again -- it seemed to be where he was the happiest in his professional life in his later years.**
****

**Tomorrow, we will gather to wish Sam a sad farewell, but he will not be forgotten. I encourage you to show your support to his wife Cheryl and family members by attending.**

**The funeral will be at 10 a.m., Saturday, February 10th at Saint Mary's Roman Catholic Church at 71 Lowell Street in Rochester, New Hampshire.**

**Sincerely, **

**Jeanie**

**REPUBLICAN SPOTLIGHT**
**This week we remember former State Senator Sam Cataldo - a great patriot and friend to many in our party.**
****

**Senator Sam Cataldo was a retired nuclear engineer and Air Force veteran serving his second term in the Senate. He represented the District 6 towns of Alton, Barnstead, Farmington, Gilmanton, and New Durham, as well as the City of Rochester. Sen. Cataldo was first elected in 2012.**

**Prior to joining the Senate, Sen. Cataldo served four terms in the New Hampshire House of Representatives.**

**Sam graduated from Lowell Tech and Northeastern University. He leaves behind his wife Cheryl and five children.**

clean_body: | | nh.gop NH REPUBLICAN PARTY WEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 9th | A Note From Jeanie Dear Derek, It is with great sadness we learned this week of Senator Sam Cataldo's passing. I had the opportunity to serve with Sam in the Senate. He was a kind and caring colleague and friend who will be sorely missed. ** Even after Sam left the Senate, he was still actively involved in New Hampshire politics, attending Republican Committee meetings, Republican events, and our Executive Board meetings. One of the last times I spoke with Sam he expressed his desire to run for office again -- it seemed to be where he was the happiest in his professional life in his later years. Tomorrow, we will gather to wish Sam a sad farewell, but he will not be forgotten. I encourage you to show your support to his wife Cheryl and family members by attending. The funeral will be at 10 a.m., Saturday, February 10th at Saint Mary's Roman Catholic Church at 71 Lowell Street in Rochester, New Hampshire. Sincerely, Jeanie Join the Grassroots Circle! The Grassroots Circle is a unique way for you to get involved while supporting the Party. Becoming a member couldn't be easier -- join today for as little as $10 per month. Photo credit: Alan Glassman REPUBLICAN SPOTLIGHT This week we remember former State Senator Sam Cataldo - a great patriot and friend to many in our party. Senator Sam Cataldo was a retired nuclear engineer and Air Force veteran serving his second term in the Senate. He represented the District 6 towns of Alton, Barnstead, Farmington, Gilmanton, and New Durham, as well as the City of Rochester. Sen. Cataldo was first elected in 2012. Prior to joining the Senate, Sen. Cataldo served four terms in the New Hampshire House of Representatives. Sam graduated from Lowell Tech and Northeastern University. body:
Hi,
The Trump Administration wants to open the pathway to offshore drilling off of our coast. To that we are saying, "Hold on a minute. Not here. Not in Rhode Island." Protecting Rhode Island's resources is the right thing to do, and the benefits help the environment and the economy.
We've already seen other politicians show they won't stand up to Trump, that they won't move Rhode Island's economy or environment forward. **That 's why I need your help to make sure Rhode Island continues standing up for what's right.**
**[Click here to send $12 or as much as you can to help ensure my campaign has the resources to win.](https://act.myngp.com/el/V_JNe45yZdQwbo-OqpnAnB3NOZFrH31ITpQD6QK5tQI=/g3WOXwq3SafqtgFv6BUJ7MsfuWWnaOUtZOVENNybdgY=?refcode=18210)**
We've made renewable energy a focus of my administration. Since 2014, we have added 5,000 green jobs -- an increase of 66%. Since 2016, we have more than doubled the amount of renewable energy used by our grid -- so let's keep going!
Earlier this week, I announced an ambitious new goal: to more than double our renewable energy use _again_ , by adding 400 more megawatts from renewable energy to our grid by the end of the summer.
We will keep going, we will keep fighting, and we will keep doing what's right. Thank you for standing with me.
Many thanks,
Gina
**[P.S. Click here to send $12 or as much as you can.](https://act.myngp.com/el/V_JNe45yZdQwbo-OqpnAnB3NOZFrH31ITpQD6QK5tQI=/g3WOXwq3SafqtgFv6BUJ7MsfuWWnaOUtZOVENNybdgY=?refcode=18210)**

name: The White House
subject: Resolute Reads
body:
1600 Daily REAL NEWS PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=be49bf1a78d4c15abfbddf2dd3702bb141a3cefc69a621034dccf121d8cc4d2058772a073494d47aab17d9e248609b5e12f95fef401cf02c
U.S. jobless claims drop to near 45-year low

- Yahoo News

The numbers of Americans filing for unemployment benefits fell last week, “dropping to its lowest level in nearly 45 years,” Yahoo News reports. This drop follows last week’s numbers from the U.S. Department of Labor, which also showed “strong job growth momentum.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=be49bf1a78d4c15ab25360290c45c502add8be3cb44a056b96d3849cbcf9dbc26169d1a9bf27b71a85dd7965370fe5bc48909a0c63f15d9d

Trump’s approval rating tops Obama’s at same point in presidency

- The Washington Times

According to the latest Rasmussen Reports, President Donald J. Trump’s approval rating is now higher than President Barack Obama’s was on the same date of his first term. “These approval polls reveal a public that has tuned out the mainstream media’s nattering against the president,” Victor Morton writes.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=be49bf1a78d4c15a369dbace4f30ef817c39a79243d6e77956cab889fb6febc86e6da8873293050dfe2865bbdd0c655777c74367ee64e080
Food stamp recipients drop under Trump

- The Daily Caller

The U.S. Department of Agriculture released new data this month showing that the number of individuals in the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) has continued to decline under the Trump Administration.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=be49bf1a78d4c15af75507cb7e3e25a5ab5d3a534fceedd08f23e432fcb1f5858fc8002df7e2ed2062226c80033cb8eae547921d6453eafc

DISH bringing over 200 new jobs to Tulsa

- News on 6

On Tuesday, satellite provider DISH announced that it is bringing about 1,000 jobs back to the U.S. from overseas—with 250 of those jobs coming to Tulsa, Oklahoma. These new jobs include roles in sales, human resources, and customer relations.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=be49bf1a78d4c15a6edbb1ac980fe247b3d626b2fc65fa91b0449fca8726d88306c5a24a0e14b179eb7ff88b46c1ab2f75a75a6d622e5471
Polls: Strong public support for 'DREAM' compromise that includes top Trump priorities

- Townhall

Townhall’s Guy Benson details the strong public support behind the President’s latest immigration proposals. As the DACA deadline approaches, Benson outlines why it only makes sense for lawmakers to back President Trump’s proposal.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=be49bf1a78d4c15a787abea6770ce891eb36c72d2dc29dcd277e035bffac7158f52809340142cf5439d018985bea1b1cbefac51d96f1ecd8
No ‘amnesty surge’ on southwest border as numbers drop in January

- The Washington Times

Illegal immigration along America’s southwest border ticked down in January, The Washington Times reports. “The declines spanned both illegal immigrants caught trying to sneak in between ports of entry, and those showing up at legal checkpoints demanding entry without permission,” Stephen Dinan writes.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=be49bf1a78d4c15af7f4c35df112c23cf2e6f1d2b69562b192a653b12c7828022853c830f9965dd6a21b2529d8ca4cab9da167e05f830705
Chipotle, citing tax changes, sends bonuses, ups benefits

- Chicago Tribune

This week, Chipotle joined the ranks of companies giving out cash, stock bonuses, and improved parental leave benefits to workers following sweeping changes to the outdated U.S. tax code championed by President Trump.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=be49bf1a78d4c15a9ef7ad29351bb70942c606865bda491a3d2c8568a18c71165defc73b3801614d59ccfcc03f017fba5082aeec5038f2bc

name: Tom Perez
subject: Our strategy doesn't work without you
body:
If you're ready to make sure Republicans face some electoral consequences for their actions, chip in now:
---
Derek --

This is all pretty simple:

1. Republicans are pushing an agenda that hurts working people -- all to benefit the billionaire class.
2. We have a chance to do something about it this November by voting them out of office.

**If you're ready to do just that -- if you're ready to make sure Republicans face some electoral consequences for their actions -- chip in $3 today to help Democrats win nationwide this year.**

**[ DONATE: $3](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522156/4067b0a/55312f170/7e52c366/1281434237/VEsEAA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/make-republicans-face-consequences?amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=25+-+DONATE+3&utm_campaign=em_20180210_tp_nd&source=em_20180210_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180210_tp_nd")
[DONATE: $10](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522156/4067b0a/55312f170/7e52c367/1281434237/VEsEAw/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/make-republicans-face-consequences?amount=10&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=26+-+DONATE+10&utm_campaign=em_20180210_tp_nd&source=em_20180210_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180210_tp_nd")
[DONATE: $25](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522156/4067b0a/55312f170/7e52c364/1281434237/VEsEAg/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/make-republicans-face-consequences?amount=25&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=27+-+DONATE+25&utm_campaign=em_20180210_tp_nd&source=em_20180210_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180210_tp_nd")
[DONATE: $50](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522156/4067b0a/55312f170/7e52c365/1281434237/VEsEDQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/make-republicans-face-consequences?amount=50&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=28+-+DONATE+50&utm_campaign=em_20180210_tp_nd&source=em_20180210_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180210_tp_nd")
[DONATE: $100](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522156/4067b0a/55312f170/7e52c362/1281434237/VEsEDA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/make-republicans-face-consequences?amount=100&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=29+-+DONATE+100&utm_campaign=em_20180210_tp_nd&source=em_20180210_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180210_tp_nd")
[Or donate another amount.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522156/4067b0a/55312f170/7e52c363/1281434237/VEsFBQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/make-republicans-face-consequences?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=30+-+Or+donate+another+amount&utm_campaign=em_20180210_tp_nd&source=em_20180210_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180210_tp_nd")
** If we work together, we can win -- we proved that last year.

We helped Doug Jones pull off a historic victory in Alabama. Our investments pushed Danica Roem, Justin Fairfax, and Ralph Northam to wins in Virginia. Phil Murphy is now Governor in New Jersey, thanks in part to our support. And there are many, many more.

We've got a winning strategy, but that strategy requires your participation. Without millions of Democrats coming together to fight back, we won't be able to take back Congress and win this year.

That's the only way we can truly stop the terrible, divisive agenda that Trump and the Republicans have laid out -- we _have_ to win in November.

**[We can't do it without you -- there is too much at stake. Contribute $3 today to show you stand with Democrats.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522156/4067b0a/55312f170/7e52c360/1281434237/VEsFBA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/make-republicans-face-consequences?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=31+-+We+cant+do+it+without+you++there+is+too+&utm_campaign=em_20180210_tp_nd&source=em_20180210_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180210_tp_nd")**

Thanks,

Tom

Tom Perez
Chair
Democratic National Committee

This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522156/4067b0a/55312f170/7e52c36c/1281434237/VEsFAA/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=35+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180210_tp_nd&source=em_20180210_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180210_tp_nd"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

name: Ben Jealous
subject: [SURVEY] What issue is most important to you?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
body:
#
Friends,
In 2012, Maryland had a bounty of civil rights ambitions: we were going to pass the DREAM Act, abolish the death penalty, pass marriage equality, expand voting rights, and pass sensible gun safety reform. It was quite the undertaking, but that didn't stop Maryland.
We went for it, and we won all of them. Because we know our individual movements are stronger together.
That's the spirit this state needs. A spirit to make our existing movements stronger by combining efforts to defend our communities, our healthcare, our environment, our working families, students, and seniors.
**[I hope you'll share a little bit about what moves and motivates you by clicking below and taking a short survey, to help me better serve the people of this state.](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a700/7ab0e2c4/7f4e2594/349a2182/852177722/VEsH/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/signup/what-matters-to-you?source=OHQ02108A")**
Onward,
Ben
